zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice de Beauchamp於大約1279年出生在英國時，她的父親William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick43歲，母親Maud FitzJohn44歲。她於1324年與William de Bruley在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1326年在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrice de Beauchamp于大约1279年出生在英国时，她的父亲William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick43岁，母亲Maud FitzJohn44岁。她于1324年与William de Bruley在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1326年在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice de Beauchamp was born about 1279, in England, her father, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, was 43 and her mother, Maud FitzJohn, was 44. She married William de Bruley in 1324, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1326, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

Elle a épousé William de Bruley en 1324, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1326, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Beauchampが約1279年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwickは43歳，母Maud FitzJohnは44歳であった。1324年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでWilliam de Bruleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1326年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1279년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud FitzJohn(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 1324년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 William de Bruley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1326년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1279, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Maud FitzJohn, tinha 44 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Bruley em 1324, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1326, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 48 anos.

ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrice de Beauchamp родилась около 1279 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Maud FitzJohn, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Bruley в 1324 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1326 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет.
